objective: Repair and re-verify the live object runtime binding between WRK-C1 and the Claude provider executor under BEM-948 P0: resolve residual merge-conflict markers in governance proof files, produce a clean idempotent transport record for the new trace, and confirm idempotent Telegram routing.
assumptions: routing.json v6 designates claude as executor.active and executor.primary; no provider switch is required for this repair cycle.

task_type 'object_runtime_binding' is absent from role_sequence.json; the cycle follows the 'default_development' sequence (analyst -> auditor -> executor -> auditor -> curator_summary) per default_task_type.

governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json contain unresolved git merge-conflict markers from the previous trace (bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z) and must be repaired before the new trace is considered clean.
Idempotency key for Telegram routing is trace_id; a second dispatch with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_repair_20260620T0935Z must produce zero new Telegram messages and zero duplicate transport records.
Telegram delivery in this cycle is synthetic (send_synthetic mode); no live bot token or production credentials are required.
steps: STEP-1 | Resolve merge-conflict markers: clean governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json by retaining the most-recent post-conflict values and removing all conflict markers; validate both files as strict JSON before proceeding.

STEP-2 | Assert WRK-C1 binding: verify routing.json executor.active == 'claude'; create governance/proofs/BEM948_binding_repair_plan.json with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_repair_20260620T0935Z as the authoritative plan artifact for this cycle.

STEP-3 | Execute object runtime binding probe: append exactly one binding_result record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with fields trace_id, cycle_id, role=executor, provider=claude, status=completed, binding_mode=live_object, telegram_idempotent=true, completed_at; skip append if record with same (trace_id, record_type) already exists.

STEP-4 | Idempotent Telegram routing verification: replay the same trace_id dispatch and assert results.jsonl gains zero additional records for this trace_id; confirm no second Telegram outbox payload is emitted; set telegram_idempotency=pass in the transport record.

STEP-5 | Report and commit: write executor report to governance/reports/bem948_p0_binding_repair_20260620T0935Z.md with checklist, changed files, checks run, and commit SHA; append handoff record to results.jsonl with from_role=executor, to_role=auditor; commit all changed governance files in one atomic commit.
acceptance: AC-1 | governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json contain zero git merge-conflict markers and parse as valid strict JSON after STEP-1.

AC-2 | governance/transport/results.jsonl contains exactly one record with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_repair_20260620T0935Z and record_type=binding_result after STEP-3; a second dispatch with the same trace_id adds zero additional records, confirming idempotency.
AC-3 | Telegram routing is idempotent: two sends with the same trace_id produce exactly one outbox entry; transport record contains telegram_idempotent=true.
risks: RISK-1 | Concurrent runners may re-introduce conflict markers into governance proof files before the repair commit is pushed; mitigated by atomic commit of all repaired files in a single step.

RISK-2 | results.jsonl append-only model has no atomic write guarantee under parallel GitHub Actions runners; simultaneous appends may interleave lines; mitigated by the single-executor-per-cycle constraint in role_sequence.json.

RISK-3 | task_type object_runtime_binding is not in role_sequence.json; if the orchestrator rejects the default_development fallback, the cycle cannot proceed; mitigated by explicit curator pre-approval recorded in the task description.

RISK-4 | provider_status.json claude.last_outcome was failed in prior traces; if the live binding probe surfaces a failure, executor must trigger fallback to gpt_codex per routing.json executor.fallback_chain before proceeding.
